summary: A monster July 2 1994 show with Help‑Slip‑Frank, Desolation Row and Smokestack Lightning soaring; recording sounds clean despite unspecified source.
review: DeadCoMule hails this July 2 1994 performance as the best of the year, praising a nonstop groove that launches from a tight Music Never Stopped/Sugaree sandwich into a breathtaking second set highlighted by Help‑Slip‑Frank, a ferocious Smokestack Lightning and a soaring Stella Blue. No recording flaws are mentioned, implying a listenable, high‑quality capture.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed 5‑star review praises an exceptional show with many standout moments and notes no audio problems, leading to a high rating despite limited sample size.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewer does not comment on technical issues; absence of complaints suggests the recording is clear and listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows with relentless momentum, opening strong and maintaining energy through a powerful second‑set sandwich of Help‑Slip‑Frank, extended drumming/space journeys, and a climactic Stella Blue.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia delivers breathtaking solos, seamlessly bridges Space into Stella Blue, and drives the show’s fireworks when everything clicks.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir is described as the captain of the first set, delivering creative chords, wonderful vocals on Desolation Row, and pulling Truckin' into a snarling Smokestack Lightning.
band_member_comments.Drums: The Drums > Space segment is praised for its expansive, imaginative journey through varied realms.
